title: teams — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by TeamsServiceProvider::boot(). All rules are used both by the observer chain (for authoritative refusal on write) AND by FormRequest data classes (for early request-time validation with clear error surfaces).

rules.name: valid_hex_color
rules.description: Value matches ^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$ — a 7-character hex color literal. Used for primary_color + secondary_color.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a 7-character hex color (e.g. #1a2b3c).

rules.name: valid_e164_phone
rules.description: Value is a valid E.164 phone number (^\+[1-9]\d{1,14}$). Used for trialist_phone.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid international phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234).

rules.name: valid_url
rules.description: Value is a valid https URL. Used for logo_url.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid HTTPS URL.

rules.name: valid_gender_category
rules.description: Value is one of: male / female / mixed / any. Backed by the GenderCategory enum cast.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be one of: male, female, mixed, any.

rules.name: valid_age_range
rules.description: When both min_age and max_age are set: refuse when min_age > max_age; refuse either value outside [3, 100]. Passes when both are null (no age constraint).
rules.default_message: The team's age range is invalid: min_age must be <= max_age, and both must be between 3 and 100.

rules.name: valid_capacity
rules.description: capacity is null OR (>=1 AND <=500). capacity_waitlist is null OR (>=0 AND <=100).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be null or a positive integer within the platform limits (capacity: 1-500, waitlist: 0-100).

rules.name: valid_schedule_pattern
rules.description: Validates the schedule_pattern JSONB blob against schemas/team.schema.json (schedulePattern sub-shape). Shape: { monday: [{ starts: 'HH:MM', ends: 'HH:MM' }], ..., sunday: [...] }. Each window: starts + ends are 'HH:MM' 24h strings; ends > starts; no overlapping windows within a day.
rules.default_message: The :attribute is not a valid schedule pattern. Each day must have non-overlapping HH:MM–HH:MM windows.

rules.name: valid_result_shape
rules.description: Validates the EventTeam.result JSONB against a sport-agnostic pattern set. Accepts one of: (1) score shape `{ score: { home: int, away: int }, ... }`; (2) position shape `{ position: int, ... }`; (3) points shape `{ points: int, ... }`; (4) mixed shape combining any of the above. Refuses arbitrary top-level keys not in the recognised catalogue.
rules.default_message: The result blob doesn't match a recognised shape. See docs/team-event-results.md for the pattern catalogue.

rules.name: team_member_polymorphic_target_exists
rules.description: Defensive rule — verifies (member_type, member_id) resolves to a live row of the declared type when the type has a registered resolver in config('teams.polymorphism.member_type_registry'). Skipped for pre-Wave-3 types (athlete, coach, staff) that don't have a resolver yet — the row is written as a placeholder + reconciled when the parent module lands.
rules.default_message: The team member reference (:member_type, :member_id) doesn't resolve to a live row.
rules.note: The rule runs at request-validation time (early feedback) AND is re-checked at observer time (authoritative). Discrepancy indicates a race — the row was deleted between validation and write.
